verdict.summary: Guide is largely accurate for EU/EEA/Swiss students, but it has a couple of likely stale/incorrect official URLs and misses some useful caveats around fines, EHIC/SNS coverage, and non-EU Erasmus students/family members.

issues.problem: This looks like an obsolete or malformed gov.pt/ePortugal URL: it uses the old www2.gov.pt host and includes raw slashes in 'ue/eee/suica', which are likely interpreted as path separators rather than part of the service slug.
issues.suggestion: Replace with the current ePortugal service URL for the permanent residence certificate, preferably on eportugal.gov.pt, or remove the direct URL and cite the service name if the slug is unstable.

issues.problem: AIMA pages for residence topics commonly sit under the '/pt/viver/...' path; this URL may be stale or missing a path segment. Because this is the key official source for the article, it should be rechecked.
issues.suggestion: Verify the live AIMA URL and, if needed, update to the current AIMA page for 'Certificado de Registo para nacionais da UE' under the 'Viver em Portugal' section.

issues.current: За неподачу запроса на Certificado de Registo в установленный срок Lei 37/2006 предусматривает coima.
issues.problem: Correct, but incomplete: the guide warns about a fine without giving the statutory range, which is useful and easy to verify.
issues.suggestion: Add the amount: 'За неподачу запроса в срок предусмотрена coima от 400 € до 1500 €.'

issues.current: EHIC — Cartão Europeu de Seguro de Doença — или частная медицинская страховка
issues.problem: This is broadly correct for many EU students, but it can be misleading if read as 'EHIC is always enough for all healthcare/residence situations'. EHIC is mainly for medically necessary care during a temporary stay/study period and does not replace registration in the Portuguese system if the person starts working or becomes covered by Portuguese social security.
issues.suggestion: Write: 'EHIC/CESD, если вы остаётесь застрахованы в стране ЕС и приехали временно на учёбу, либо частная страховка; если начнёте работать в Португалии, медицинское покрытие обычно переходит через Segurança Social/SNS.'

issues.current: EU/Erasmus-студент: Certificado de Registo вместо визы D4
issues.problem: The body correctly says this applies to EU/EEA/Swiss citizens, but the title may be read as applying to any Erasmus student. Erasmus status alone does not exempt a third-country national from visa/residence rules; non-EU Erasmus students may still need D4 or another appropriate route depending on nationality, duration and host programme.
issues.suggestion: Change title to something like: 'Гражданин ЕС/EEE/Швейцарии на Erasmus или учёбе: CRUE вместо D4'.

missing_topics: Clarify explicitly that non-EU/EEA/Swiss Erasmus students are outside this guide and may need D4/residence procedures via AIMA.
Mention that non-EU family members of an EU student do not get CRUE at Câmara Municipal; they normally need a residence card for family members of an EU citizen via AIMA.
Add the statutory coima range for late/non-registration under Lei 37/2006.
Add a short caveat on EHIC/CESD versus SNS/Segurança Social coverage if the student starts working in Portugal.
